titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Add. Ch. 76697

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=81 status=live modified=2021-04-04 14:26:54.679332+00 | translation moa=82 status=live modified=2021-04-04 14:26:58.109502+00

div.p:
W. dei gratia Rex Scottorum Episcopis . abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis . Vicecomitibus prepositis
Ministris . Ceterisque probis hominibus tocius terre sue salutem. Sciant presentes et futuri me concessisse et hac carta mea confirmasse Orm filio Hugonis et heredibus suis
abbaciam de Abernithi cum omnibus iustis pertinentiis suis et rectitudinibus eidem
abbacie iuste pertinentibus sic fuit anno et die quo Rex Dauid auus meus fuit uiuus
et mortuus in bosco et plano in pratis et pascuis Moris et aquis . Viis et Semitis . Sta
gnis et molendinis . exceptis .x. libratis terre quas dedi Henrico Reuel cum fi
lia predicti Orm. Tenend’ sibi et heredibus suis de me et de heredibus meis
in feudo et hereditate libere et quiete ab omnibus seruiciis et consuetudinibus excep
to comuni auxilio . Comuni exercitu . Comuni operatione . Cum Socca et
Sacca . Cum tol et them et infangenthef . ita tamen quod non habeat furcam neque fos
sam nisi in duobus locis . scilicet apud Abernithi . et apud Inuerarecthin . Reddendo
mihi inde et heredibus meis singulis annis .xx. libras argenti . Scilicet .x. libras
ad festum Sancti Iohannis baptiste . et x. libras ad festum Sancte Brigide. Volo etiam ut
homines sui de Fif et de Gouerin ueniant ad fossam de Abernithi. De aliis uero terris
suis ueniant ad fossam de Inuerarecthin. Testibus Matheo episcopo de Aberdeon . Andrea
episcopo de Caten’. Hugone capellano meo . Comite Dunecano . Comite Gileberto . Hugone Giffard .
Nesso filio Willelmi . Galfrido de Maleuill’ . Waltero de Berkel’. Willelmo de Mor
temer . Willelmo de Lindesia. Apud Pert.

William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and other worthy men of his entire land greeting. Let present and future know me to have conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to Orm son of Hugh and his heirs the abbey of Abernethy with all its just pertinents and rights justly pertaining to the same abbey of Abernethy as it was in the year and on the day that King David my grandfather was alive and dead; in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, moors and waters, roads and paths, millponds and mills; except for 10 pounds’ worth of land that I gave to Henry Revel with the daughter of the foresaid Orm. For him and his heirs to hold of me and my heirs in feu and heritage, free and quit from all services and customs except for common aid, common army, and common work; with soke and sake, with toll and team and infangthief, yet so that he shall not have gallows nor pit except in two places, that is, at Abernethy, and at Inverarity. With 20 pounds of silver to be rendered to me and my heirs from there, that is, 10 pounds at the feast of Saint John the Baptist, and 10 pounds at the feast of Saint Brigid. I also will that his own men of Fife and Gowrie come to the pit at Abernethy; for his other lands, however, let them come to the pit at Inverarity. As witnesses: Matthew bishop of Aberdeen; Andrew bishop of Caithness; Hugh my chaplain; Earl Duncan; Earl Gilbert; Hugh Giffard; Ness son of William; Geoffrey de Malville; Walter of Berkeley; William de Mortimer; William of Lindsay. At Perth.
